title: MechanicDB: OBD-II Diagnostic & Repair Database
subtitle: Automotive OBD-II trouble codes with ranked repair procedures & cost estimates
description:
# MechanicDB: OBD-II Diagnostic & Repair Database

Relational database mapping 15,886 OBD-II trouble codes — 9,249 SAE-standard plus 6,637 manufacturer-specific across 32 makes — to 56,561 ranked repair procedures across 647 authored fault families, with DIY difficulty ratings and 75,055 aftermarket parts mappings with cost estimates. This free Kaggle release contains a 90-code curated sample (75 SAE + 15 OEM).

## Provenance & Methodology

Code definitions are compiled from public SAE J2012-derived sources (MIT-licensed compilation, attribution preserved); repair procedures, DIY difficulty ratings, and cost ranges are original authored content grounded in standard diagnostic practice. Every release is built by a deterministic, test-gated pipeline (anti-template uniqueness checks, referential integrity, no-fabricated-codes gate).

Sample source, full documentation, and data dictionary: https://github.com/MechanicDB/MechanicDB-public - the full commercial dataset (15,886 codes) is available under license; see the repository README.

resources.description: Master registry of standardized SAE-universal and manufacturer-specific (OEM) Diagnostic Trouble Codes.

fields.name: code_id
fields.description: Primary key identifier for the trouble code record.

fields.name: dtc_code
fields.description: Standardized 5-character OBD-II trouble code (e.g., P0171, P0300, P0420).

fields.name: system_category
fields.description: Automotive system classification (Powertrain, Chassis, Body, Network).

fields.name: is_oem_specific
fields.description: Boolean flag: 0 = SAE-universal, 1 = manufacturer-specific.

fields.name: oem_make
fields.description: Manufacturer display name for OEM rows (e.g., Ford, Chevrolet, BMW); empty for SAE-universal rows.

fields.name: fault_family
fields.description: Slug of the authored fault family this code belongs to (groups codes sharing a diagnosis and repair path).

fields.name: short_description
fields.description: Concise summary of the diagnostic trouble code.

fields.name: detailed_technical_explanation
fields.description: In-depth technical explanation of what the engine control module detects.

resources.description: Ranked repair procedures and cost estimation matrices for trouble codes.

fields.name: fix_id
fields.description: Primary key identifier for the repair procedure.

fields.name: code_id
fields.description: Foreign key referencing dtc_codes.code_id.

fields.name: fix_title
fields.description: Actionable title of the recommended diagnostic inspection or repair.

fields.name: probability_rank
fields.description: Likelihood rank (1 = most common/probable root cause).

fields.name: difficulty_level
fields.description: Skill requirement rating (Easy DIY, Moderate DIY, Professional Required).

fields.name: est_parts_cost_min_usd
fields.description: Estimated minimum aftermarket parts cost in US dollars.

fields.name: est_parts_cost_max_usd
fields.description: Estimated maximum aftermarket parts cost in US dollars.

fields.name: est_labor_hours
fields.description: Average professional mechanic labor hours required.

fields.name: step_by_step_instructions
fields.description: Bulleted step-by-step diagnostic and repair instructions.

resources.description: Common aftermarket replacement parts mapped to diagnostic fixes.

fields.name: part_id
fields.description: Primary key identifier for the replacement part.

fields.name: fix_id
fields.description: Foreign key referencing diagnostic_fixes.fix_id.

fields.name: part_name
fields.description: Name or description of the replacement part/kit.

fields.name: amazon_search_query
fields.description: Direct search URL for acquiring the part on Amazon or auto parts catalogs.

resources.description: Pre-joined analytical view combining trouble codes, ranked fixes, and cost estimates.
